title: Major steps forward in government office estate rationalisation
body: "The Government Property Agency (GPA) has closed the doors at another two London properties as part of its estates rationalisation programme, moving a step closer to its target of £32 million cost savings this financial year.  Windsor House and Albany House closed in September and November 2024 respectively, with civil servants relocated to other properties on the existing government office estate.   The closures are part of the Whitehall Campus Programme, which aims to reduce the central London estate by 55% and create a campus of high-quality, shared workspaces that enable smarter working and provide better value for money.  Read the full story on the GPA website. "
